search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG12 dysphagia head neck oral cancer symptoms recommendations
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic endoscopy, imaging, swallowing study, diet, referral, or urgency
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The source supplies recognition context only and does not establish a diagnosis.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with swallowing symptoms; age-specific recommendation thresholds and acute airway or neurological presentations require separate pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary, urgent, audiology, respiratory, sleep, or ENT assessment as qualified by the reviewed source and workflow purpose.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE ENT, audiology, sleep-service, emergency, prescribing, referral, and local pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official sections and current source pages were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on its issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact dysphagia section supports documenting swallowing symptoms, duration, impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician concern in recommendation-qualified contexts without asserting cancer or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of dysphagia, weight-loss combinations, and jaundice in recommendation-specific age and symptom contexts; it does not assert cancer or an automatic referral decision.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact head-and-neck section supports clinician-performed ENT and neck assessment and review of voice, oral, or neck features without asserting malignancy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of recommendation-qualified persistent unexplained hoarseness, unexplained or persistent neck lump, oral ulceration lasting more than 3 weeks, lip or oral-cavity lump, red or red-and-white oral patch, and unexplained thyroid lump. It does not assert cancer or generate referral.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact safety-net section supports documenting an entered plan, safety net, review, and questions without generating a referral or interval.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

unresolved_source_gaps: Oropharyngeal versus oesophageal symptoms, aspiration, neurological disease, nutrition, acute obstruction, investigation, diagnosis, and management remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
